subject: Biology
title: Antibiotic Resistance
description: A quick win: understand Antibiotic Resistance.

hook.text: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928. It saved millions. But bacteria are fighting back.

buildup.text: Overuse of antibiotics in medicine and farming gives resistant bacteria an advantage.

discovery.text: MRSA—methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus—shrugs off drugs that once killed it easily.

twist.text: Bacteria share resistance genes with each other, even across species, spreading immunity fast.

climax.text: Without new antibiotics, routine surgeries and minor infections could become deadly again.

punchline.text: We gave bacteria a challenge. They adapted.

quiz.question: How do bacteria spread antibiotic resistance?
options: By sharing resistance genes across species
